references.title: Early Warning System for Online STEM Learning—A Slimmer Approach Using Recurrent Neural Networks

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Passing the course in a specific week
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Data is collected every week and concatenated to the previous sequence.
extracted.Models: SRN, LSTM, GRU, MLP, CNN, Logistic Regression, SVM, Decision Tree, Random Forest
extracted.Comments:
Authors do everyweek prediction using a dataset from an online course.

Authors observe that predictive performance tends to increase as the number of weeks advances (which is expected).
extracted.Limitations:
Considers a general education course

Small dataset

annotations.text: CNN models are the most common models used for prediction of learning outcomes (?). Authors argue that RNN based models are more adequate for the task of identifying students at risk.

Authors use data from an Online course in a Taiwanese university. Authors extract multiple features and train their RNN models and benchmark it against other alternatives: SVM, LR, DT, RF, CNN based model and a standard MLP.
Results show that RNN surpasses conventional classifiers and achieves similar performance to the CNN model. Moreover, identification is passive after a third of the semester has passed.
